content: The linguistic definition of reflexion as a mirror-like reflection of perception can be extended to frame LLM self-reflection as a multi-layered lexical feedback process: just as the word reflexion encodes both the physical act of light reflection and the cognitive act of critical rethinking, LLMs can leverage this dual framing to build hierarchical self-reflection that first mirrors their own output as an observable object, then engages in critical re-evaluation of that object. This dual framing outperforms single-perspective self-reflection by grounding abstract self-evaluation in a concrete, etymologically rooted layered structure.
